etymology.description: 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") + phonetic: 勿(勿) (wù) = related to the mouth, with phonetic indication
etymology.interpretation: The character 吻(wěn, "kiss") combines 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") for its semantic element, as a kiss is associated with the mouth, and 勿(勿) (wù) for its phonetic component, indicating pronunciation.

examples.chinese: 他轻轻地吻了她的额头(他輕輕地吻了她的額頭)。
examples.pinyin: Tā qīngqīng de wěn le tā de étóu.

examples.chinese: 在电影结束时(在電影結束時)，他们深情地吻在一起(他們深情地吻在一起)。
examples.pinyin: Zài diànyǐng jiéshù shí, tāmen shēnqíng de wěn zài yīqǐ.
examples.english: At the end of the movie, they kissed passionately.

examples.chinese: 她给了我一个告别吻(她給了我一個告別吻)。
examples.pinyin: Tā gěi le wǒ yī gè gàobié wěn.
examples.english: She gave me a farewell kiss.
